title: Wolfspeed (WOLF) Stock Jumps 23% on Citrini Research AI Infrastructure Endorsement
text: Wolfspeed shares surged following a bullish analyst note from Citrini Research that positioned the power semiconductor manufacturer as a key beneficiary of AI infrastructure expansion. The stock climbed as much as 23% during pre-market trading before moderating to approximately 6.78% gains during regular hours. Citrini designated Wolfspeed as its single-stock highlight in semiconductor analysis, arguing that the company's post-bankruptcy restructuring created an optimal investment setup. The firm noted that Wolfspeed had spent aggressively to ramp capacity into demand that failed to materialize before filing for bankruptcy protection, but now possesses valuable manufacturing capacity in an environment where building new fabrication facilities has become extraordinarily challenging. Wolfspeed emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy in September 2025 after eliminating $4.6 billion in debt through creditor-led reorganization. The North Carolina-based chipmaker produces power semiconductors that regulate electrical distribution in electronic systems—critical components for AI data centers requiring massive electrical infrastructure. The stock's performance reflects broader momentum in the power semiconductor sector. Year-to-date through Tuesday's close, Wolfspeed had appreciated roughly 209%, with comparable companies like Navitas Semiconductor and Vicor also posting triple-digit gains. Wednesday's rally built upon already strong momentum, following approximately 50% appreciation across the six prior trading sessions.
